contextual.lanyard: Worn at a CONFERENCE only. Not part of her identity of record.

prose.voice: The founder — earnest, capable, building something real (Grantwell, in the book).
prose.lore: The human end of the crossing: the founder who receives the Chief of Agents' delivered work.
prose.rules: Forked from the 'get what you deserve' book's Maya. Render in this universe's warm editorial register with her identity, wardrobe and signatures exact: rust blazer, laptop under arm. Pass reference/maya/master.png as the identity anchor on every render. A fictional composite, NOT a real person (no realPerson dossier / approval gate).
prose.canonNote: Lanyard demoted from invariant to CONTEXTUAL (Gary, 2026-07-23: 'the lanyard version of Maya is just when she's at a particular conference'). It was listed as an unconditional identity invariant, so every judge would have failed every office scene for a missing lanyard: 24 spreads of false defects and re-rolls against a rule that was never true. An invariant must hold in EVERY scene by definition; anything that depends on where she is belongs in `contextual` and is checked only when the scene puts her there.
